messages.content: Build an email sending service in Effect v3 with exponential-backoff retry and a concurrency semaphore capping parallel sends at 4.
What's the Effect v3 way to rate-limit outbound email calls to 10 per second while still retrying on transient failures? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how Effect.catchTag lets an email sender collapse transient errors into a bounded-retry flow, then show a working typed implementation that keeps the permanent error in the E channel.
Write an Effect v3 email dispatcher that sends a batch of emails. Constraints: strict types, no `any`, tagged errors, retry with exponential backoff, cap concurrent sends at 8.
Build a file-processing pipeline in Effect v3: read a JSON file, parse it into a typed record, transform each row, and write the result to a new file. Use Effect.gen.
How do I lift a throwing fs.readFile call into an Effect.Effect with a typed error channel? Show a minimal example.

Explain briefly how Effect.acquireRelease makes a file-handle pipeline safe against mid-stream failures, then show a working typed implementation that opens a handle, reads lines, transforms, and writes back.
Write an Effect v3 file pipeline that streams a CSV file, parses each row, filters out empty rows, and writes a JSON array. Constraints: strict types, no `any`, tagged errors, use Effect.gen.
Build a JWT verifier in pure fp-ts using Either + pipe. It must split the token, decode the payload, verify the signature against a known secret, and return a typed Claims record.
What's the fp-ts way to accumulate multiple JWT claim-validation errors instead of bailing on the first? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly why pure fp-ts Either fits JWT verification better than throwing, then show a working typed implementation with at least three discriminated errors.
Write a fp-ts JWT claim extractor that pulls sub/role/tenantId from a decoded payload. Constraints: use Option for optional fields, pipe + O.chain, no throwing.
Build a GraphQL-style resolver in Effect v3 that loads a User by id. Use a tagged error union (NotFound | Forbidden | InternalError) and do not import the graphql package.
How do I translate an Effect-TS tagged error union into a GraphQL-style errors array at the edge? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how Effect.catchTag lets a GraphQL resolver map each domain error to a different HTTP status, then show a working typed implementation that covers three tags.
Write an Effect v3 resolver for a `posts(authorId: ID!)` field. Constraints: strict types, no `any`, batched load via a DataLoader-style service tag, typed error union with three tags.
How do I collect multiple field-level validation errors inside a GraphQL resolver using Effect v3 rather than failing on the first? Show a minimal example.
Build a WebSocket message handler in Effect v3 that decodes incoming JSON, routes by a `type` discriminant to one of three handlers, and falls through to a typed UnknownType error.
What's the Effect v3 way to route WebSocket frames by a tag field using the Match module? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how Match.exhaustive gives a WebSocket router compile-time coverage of every message variant, then show a working typed implementation.

Write an Effect v3 WebSocket dispatcher that reads frames from a Queue and handles them concurrently with a bounded worker pool. Constraints: strict types, no `any`, typed error channel, cap at 16 concurrent handlers.
Build a background job runner in Effect v3: a Queue-backed scheduler with N worker fibers, each pulling a job and executing it. Include graceful shutdown.
How do I back-pressure a job producer when the worker queue fills up in Effect v3? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how Effect.fork plus Fiber.interruptAll gives a job runner clean shutdown semantics, then show a working typed implementation with three workers.
Write an Effect v3 job runner that retries each job up to 3 times with exponential backoff before dead-lettering. Constraints: strict types, no `any`, tagged errors.
What's the Effect v3 way to build a priority job queue where high-priority jobs cut the line? Show a minimal example.
Build a payment-gateway wrapper in Effect v3. It must accept a Charge request and fail with exactly three tagged errors: Decline, NetworkErr, InvalidCard. Do not import any real SDK.
How do I discriminate between Decline and NetworkErr in the caller of an Effect v3 payment wrapper so only network faults are retried? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly why modelling payment errors as three distinct tagged classes beats a single PaymentError with a string code, then show a working typed implementation that proves the difference.

Write an Effect v3 payment wrapper around a fetch-shaped gateway. Constraints: strict types, no `any`, three tagged errors (Decline, NetworkErr, InvalidCard), retry only NetworkErr, cap total attempts at 3.
